channel.title: Apple Bitz XL w/ Brian Tong
channel.description: Apple Bitz XL is everything good and bad inside the world of Apple from tech host Brian Tong. He brings on special guests and gives his raw honest take while having fun after covering and working for Apple for over 20 years. Support the show! www.patreon.com/briantong

item.title: MacBook Neo Hands-On Impressions! Plus, Apple's Spring Announcements (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 370)
item.description: It's my first impressions with Apple's new entry-level MacBook Neo that surprises with its $599 price point, and drops to $499 for education. Plus, thoughts on the new M5 MacBook Air, M5 Pro/Max MacBook Pro and Studio Display family. And, the new iPhone 17e and M4 iPad Air. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: Apple's March 4th MacBook Event Is Official! Plus, Apple Intelligence Is Delayed...Again (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 369)
item.description:
Apple's first event of 2026 is official! We expect to see the new A18 Pro entry-level MacBook and new M5 Pro and M5 Max MacBook Pros, but could there be more surprises? Plus, Apple drops the ball...again, with the revamped Siri and Apple Intelligence.

item.title: Apple AirTag (2nd Gen) Review, Two New Versions of Siri & Apple's Earnings Set Records...Again (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 368)
item.description: Apple's first new product of 2026 is the new AirTag 2nd generation. Are the upgrades worth upgrading for? Or getting for the first time? Plus, two new versions of Siri with the Google Gemini-backed Apple Intelligence are coming. And, Apple has crushed its earnings reports with another all-time record. Learn more about your ad choices. item.title: Apple Announces Google Gemini Will Power The Next-Gen Siri This Year (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 367)
item.description: After all the rumors, Apple officially says that Google Gemini will be the foundation of the next Siri and Apple Intelligence, with new Apple Intelligence features to come as well. What does it mean for Apple now, what does it mean for you and what does it mean for the future? Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: Apple 2026 Predictions Show w/ Rich DeMuro (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 366)
item.description:
We kick off the new year with Special Guest KTLA's Tech Report Rich DeMuro and our Apple 2026 predicitons. Will the iPhone Fold be their most important product? Can they show us a true inspired version of Apple Intelligence? Do consumers even care about AI? Can Apple finally take a big chunk of the smart home market? It's all that and so much more!
You can help support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU!

item.title: Why Are Automakers Dropping CarPlay? Is CarPlay Ultra In Trouble? (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 364)
item.description:
Special guest Joel Feder from TheDrive.com joins the podcast to talk about the future of Apple, and its uncertain relationship with the automotive industry. Is it now turning its back on CarPlay and CarPlay Ultra? Is Apple losing traction in another emerging space where cars are now software-defined vehicles more than ever?
GM Bets You’ll Miss CarPlay Less With Built-In Apple Music Support
https://www.thedrive.com/news/gm-bets-youll-miss-carplay-less-with-built-in-apple-music-support
Your Next EV Won’t Be Obsolete: How Rivian Is Designing for the Next Decade
https://www.thedrive.com/news/how-rivian-is-future-proofing-its-evs-7-10-years-out
You can help support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU!

item.title: Apple's Smart Home Hub, AirTags 2 & Studio Display 2 Leak! Plus, Apple's Talent Exodus - Update! (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 363)
item.description:
Apple releases iOS 26.2 and the latest software updates for its entire lineup. Three new Apple products and a mystery fourth one get leaked by internal code and show us what's coming in 2026. Plus, the major shake-up with leadership and talent leaving Apple puts them at a crossroads. Where do they go from here? Is it a good thing? Or is Apple in trouble?
You can help support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU!

item.title: What's Next For iOS 27? Android & iPhone Can Now AirDrop! (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 362)
item.description:
Android and Apple can now seamlessly AirDrop & Quick Share files between devices. Is this a sign of more collaborations to come? iPhone Pocket sold out because they barely made any. What's next for iOS 27? And will the 'iPhone Fold' be too expensive?

item.title: Apple's iPhone Pocket...Really? Plus, Tesla Is Finally Looking To Add Carplay (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 361)
item.description:
The Apple November product launch is not what you expected; it's a designer sock for your iPhone. I mean, it's the iPhone Pocket. Plus, Tesla is finally looking to bring CarPlay to its vehicles. Apple is delaying the next iPhone Air release, and a change of guard is happening within Apple management.
You can help support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU!

item.title: How Apple Built The New Spatial Persona For Vision Pro - Interview w/ Apple (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 359)
item.description:

item.title: I Used Apple Vision Pro For 45 Minutes And It Blew My Mind (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 270)
item.description: This is a special bonus episode and my raw reactions after my hands-on demo with the Apple Vision Pro from WWDC 23. Does it mean I'm going to get one? They still have 6-9 months or even more to make it even better. And even better with the help of developers and their own in-house improvements. Apple just elevated the space for everyone and the industry. It's an incredible time to be alive as a tech fan, but it will still cost $3499. What will make the Apple Headset a success? Some interesting trends came out of it. You can help support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU! item.description: Special Gues Ray Wong joins the pod after Apple drops surprise announcements right after CES 2023! The new M2 Max and M2 Pro chips bring even more power to the New MacBook Pro and Mac mini for 2023. Plus, Apple announces the New HomePod is back with a similar design, faster processor, and what they claim is even better sounding than the original. Will it sell for $299? Then Ray and I talk about how Apple's rumored 'Reality Pro' headset could end up being different than any other headset on the market. You can help support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU! item.description: Visit Indeed.com/applebitz to start hiring now. Don't blink twice. The title of the show is accurate. I got a chance to get an exclusive interview with Apple CEO Tim Cook where he talks about his education, the lesson Steve Jobs taught him, and staying calm with all eyes on Apple. It was a great opportunity, and I wanted to say thank you to all of you who have been following me on my independent journey. This is an accomplishment and I feel like it's a win for all of us. Also, iPhone 15 Pro Rumors, Google Maps for iOS get an Augmented Reality View and Windows 11 can now be run on Apple Silicon Macs! item.title: WWDC 2022 Recap & Reactions! (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 222)
item.description: Free $75 credit to boost your post at Indeed.com/applebitz. Terms and conditions apply. Offer good for a limited time Special Guest Developer Joe Cieplinski joins the pod and we go in-depth with all the announcements from WWDC 2022. I talk about being there for the very first time and iOS 16 is impressing us right out of the gates, CarPlay looks to the future and we breakdown watchOS 9, iPadOS, and macOS Ventura, There's so much here to talk about we could have gone a whole lot longer...but we didn't. item.title: Review: M1 13-inch MacBook Pro: The Game Has Changed (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 146)
item.description: FREE SEVENTY-FIVE DOLLAR CREDIT at Indeed.com/applebitz. Offer expires 12/31/20. HEADSPACE.COM/applebitz for a FREE ONE-MONTH TRIAL with access to Headspace’s full library of meditations for every situation. Special Guest Joe Cieplinski joins the pod to talk about Apple's hottest piece of hardware this year, the M1 13-inch MacBook Pro. More power than a fully loaded 16-inch MacBook Pro, nearly double the battery life and a fan that doesn't go off that often. Apple has just changed the game and the Mac looks to be one of the most exciting products we've seen in a long time. We also talk Homepod mini and Apple change to only take a 15% percent App Store royalty cut for developers that make less than $1 million a year. You can help support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU! item.description: It's all my reactions to Apple's 'WWDC 20 keynote with Special Guest Tech Reporter Rich DeMuro from KTLA. item.title: iPhone getting in-display Touch ID for...China? (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 75)
item.description: A report says Apple is planning on releasing a new iPhone with an in-display fingerprint sensor, but only in China to help drive down costs in that market. Is this baloney? Apple's using AR Kit so it looks like you're making eye contact with Facetime videos...but you aren't. Creepy, but cool. Because they should be bringing that to the US. Plus, the Jony Ive inspired Apple Car that you never wanted. You can support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU! item.description: Thanks to Grammarly for supporting Apple Bitz XL Podcast. For 20% off a Grammarly premium account, go to grammarly.com/applebitz Apple's WWDC 19 Keynote was the best one they've had in years . Special Guest Lance Ulanoff was at the keynote and tells us why Privacy was the top story for him. Apple brought their 'A' game with the New Mac Pro, the Pro Display XDR, iOS 13, iPadOS, watchOS 6, tvOS 6 and macOS Catalina. They've set the table for 2019 and a big year in software and hardware with the iPhone 11 and more coming this fall. item.title: Reactions to the Apple/Qualcomm deal, Intel drops out & iOS 13 details!(Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 63)
item.description: Thanks to Grammarly for supporting Apple Bitz XL Podcast. For 20% off a Grammarly premium account, go to grammarly.com/applebitz Apple Bitz XL Senior Legal Correspondent Gil Cabrera (@GilCabrera on twitter) joins the show to talk about the suprise deal between Apple and Qualcomm to squash their 2-plus year beef and work together again. It's a win for consumers, but the big loser? Intel shuts down their 5G modem business. New iOS 13 and macOS 10.15 details and a story about people wearing AirPods while having sex? Really? You can support this show and my independent work at www.patreon.com/briantong THANK YOU! item.title: .Apple apologizes for MacBook 'Butterfly' keyboard & AirPower canceled (Apple Bitz XL, Ep.61)
item.description: Thanks to Grammarly for supporting Apple Bitz XL Podcast. For 20% off a Grammarly premium account, go to grammarly.com/applebitz The dust has settled and cooler heads have prevailed after Apple officially canceled the AirPower. But it's still an unprecedented move and one of their biggest failures ever. Then they had to publicly apologize about the Macbook 'Butterfly' keyboards that we've been screaming about the past 4 years. Apple is still Apple, but it's not looking pretty right now. item.title: 'Tim Apple' is Apple's new CEO. Plus, Apple AR Glasses in 2020? (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 56)
item.description: Thanks to Grammarly for supporting Apple Bitz XL Podcast. For 20% off a Grammarly premium account, go to grammarly.com/applebitz Hollywood producers say Apple execs are being "intrusive" and offering criticism and suggestions to content that will be appearing on Apple's new service. Tim Cook's common note to them, "don't be so mean". Yikes, when did Apple execs start thinking they were TV & movie execs. item.title: Is Apple playing catch up to the Galaxy S10/S10+/Fold in 2019? (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 54)
item.description: Thanks EERO. Get $100 off the Eero base unit and 2 beacons package, and 1 year of Eero Plus at eero.com/applebitz and enter applebitz at checkout! After Samsung Unpacked I can't remember the last time Apple felt this far behind from an innovation standpoint. The Galaxy S10/S10+ and Samsung Fold are coming for Cupertino. Plus, Ming-Chi Kuo releases his report of what to expect from Apple with a new 16-inch MacBook Pro, a 31.6-inch Display, new iPads and iPhones and AirPods. item.title: No 5G iPhone before 2020, New AirPods in 2020? Plus, ECG comes to Apple Watch(Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 41)
item.description: Apple drops a whole bunch of software updates from iOS 12.1.1 that fixes the FaceTime flip screen button mess, and expands eSIM support. Apple’s ECG feature finally arrives on the Apple Watch and new reports claim the all-new AirPods won’t be coming until 2020, but we’ll get wireless charging in early 2019. But what about AirPower? And the iPhone looks to get 5G by 2020...at the earliest. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: Is Apple Already Throttling The 2018 MacBook Pro's Core i9 Processor (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 18)
item.description: Tech Reporter Rich DeMuro joins us for the show. Reviews for the new MacBook Pro are in and most of them are extremely positive. On the flip side, YouTuber Dave Lee has found that power users may be getting a MacBook Pro that can't handle the heat of the new i9 and is throttling its speed. New iPhone leaks as usual, and if you're single, you might want to get an iPhone. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: Apple Release New MacBook Pros! Mac Mini's Next (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 17)
item.description: Apple drops a surprise on us with new 2018 MacBook Pros that still have that horrible Touch Bar. The Mac mini is finally getting updated after nearly 4 years. The 6.1 inch LCD iPhone will be getting an edge-to-edge display like the OLED iPhone X thanks to a new chip. Sonos gets AirPlay 2 functionality for the first time ever and now there’s really no reason to get a HomePod. Plus, I know what the color orange looks like. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: Everything You Can Expect At WWDC 2018 (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 11)
item.description: All indications say WWDC 2018 is going to be a software heavy event with little to no hardware showing up this year. iOS looks to bring Digital Health, ARKit 2.0 and new Animojis, but its going to be a year of refinements and stability improvements. Maybe we'll get surprised with new Macs, but be prepared, it's all about software. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: IOS 11.4 Beta Brings AirPlay 2, Messages In ICloud & HomePod Stereo Support Closer (ABXL, Ep. 9)
item.description: The sixth beta for iOS 11.4 tells us the official release is coming soon. HomePods only made up 6% of smart speaker sales with Alibaba topping them. I didn't even know Alibaba had a smart speaker! Plus, Apple vs. Samsung is the $1 billion case that won't go away. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices

item.title: Your iPhone's 4-Digit Passcode Can Be Cracked In 6.5 Minutes By GrayKey (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 4)
item.description: A new iPhone cracking tool can unlock an iPhone's 4-digit passcode in 6.5 minutes and a 6-digit passcode in 11 hours with the GrayKey box. The next iPhone could be getting dual SIM support, Greenpeace isn't too happy about the Apple's new disassembly robot Daisy and stop snoring with your Apple Watch. Learn more about your ad choices. item.title: Apple's HomePod Sales Aren't Very Good (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 3)
item.description: Apple can't be thrilled with HomePod sales. Consumers aren't thrilled with what it can do for $349. So make a better product. item.title: Apple's New Mac Pro Is Coming In 2019 (Apple Bitz XL, Ep. 2)
item.description: Apple official announces the new modular Mac Pro is coming in 2019. Intel's latest Coffee Lake processors could bring us a six-core 15-inch Mac Book Pro, but Bloomberg reports Apple is planning to use its own chips in Macs in 2020, replacing Intel. Plus, Apple snags Google’s AI Chief. Learn more about your ad choices. Visit megaphone.fm/adchoices
